figures.caption: Table 1: Overall performance comparison. The best results are bolded, and the second best are underlined. Improvements are calculated against the best baseline. † denotes that the corresponding method is reproduced by ourselves.

Figure 1: The “False Negative” Dilemma in RecSys DPO. Left: In NLP, negatives are strictly incorrect, making DPO effective. Middle: In RecSys, hard negatives may include unobserved positives, causing mis-penalization and embedding drift. Right: Top-K sampling adds randomness among candidates, balancing positives.
Figure 5: A qualitative case study on the Amazon Toys and Games dataset.

Figure 2: Overall framework of RoDPO. (a) Multimodal Encoder: Item IDs and text/image features are embedded into a shared space, refined by a sparse MoE, and modeled by modality-specific Transformers with a temporal MoE for next-item prediction. (b) Robust DPO: Preference pairs are formed by the observed item yw and a sampled negative yl from a dynamic top-K pool (excluding yw), optimized against a frozen reference policy.
